channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police confirm no rape occurred outside Epsom church after investigation
item.description: Surrey Police have concluded their investigation into a reported rape outside a church in Epsom earlier this month, confirming that no sexual offence took place. The police investigation followed publ...

item.title: Sara Cox appointed new host of BBC Radio 2 breakfast show following Scott Mills departure
item.description: BBC DJ Sara Cox has been appointed as the new host of Radio 2's breakfast show, replacing Scott Mills. Cox, originally from Bolton, has been presenting Radio 2's teatime show since 2019 and expressed ...

item.title: Kimani Ichung’wah reveals why he blocked Uhuru Kenyatta after a heated argument
item.description: Kikuyu MP Kimani Ichung’wah recently made shocking revelations during an interview with Oga Obinna. The politician disclosed that he was introduced to President William Ruto by former President Uhuru ...

item.title: China Announces Expansion of Island Facilities and Plans for Nuclear Aircraft Carrier
item.description: China has released a video suggesting the development of its fourth aircraft carrier, potentially nuclear-powered, alongside plans to continue expanding its claimed islands. The video, titled 'Into th...

item.title: Olly Robbins Prevented Cabinet Office from Accessing Mandelson's Vetting Summary
item.description: Cat Little, a senior civil servant in the Cabinet Office, has revealed that Olly Robbins, the former head of the Foreign Office, refused to provide a summary of Peter Mandelson's security vetting to h...

item.title: Toronto Subway Musicians Enliven Daily Commutes with Live Performances
item.description: Toronto's subway stations are home to a vibrant community of musicians who perform regularly for commuters. Among them is cellist Leo Zhang, who has played underground for nearly 30 years, offering pe...

item.title: Ramaphosa Weighs Action Against Police Commissioner Masemola Amid SAPS Scandal
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is under mounting pressure to decide the future of National Police Commissioner Fannie Masemola, who has become entangled in a criminal case linked to an ongoing scandal with...

item.title: Labor government delays gambling advertising reforms despite Murphy report recommendations
item.description: The Australian Labor government’s proposed reforms to gambling advertising have faced widespread criticism. Over 1,000 days have passed since the 2023 Murphy report, chaired by the late MP Peta Murphy...

item.title: Jomo Kenyatta Lookalike Arrested in Fuel Prices Protest in Nairobi
item.description: A striking resemblance between Michael Ngige, a protester arrested during a demonstration against high fuel prices in Nairobi, and Kenya's first president, Mzee Jomo Kenyatta, has sparked widespread r...

item.title: Rising Woman Initiative closes sixth season after expanding impact on women’s leadership in Tanzania
item.description: The Citizen Rising Woman Initiative (RWI) has officially concluded its sixth season, marking another important milestone in promoting women’s leadership and empowerment across Tanzania and beyond. Run...

item.title: High Voter Turnout in Bengal and Trump's Controversial Remarks on India
item.description: In the first phase of assembly elections in West Bengal, voter turnout reached an impressive 91.6% by 6 pm, reflecting strong participation from the electorate across 152 constituencies. Tamil Nadu re...

item.title: Kidnappers of Lagos Woman Demand Ransom in US Dollars
item.description: In a disturbing development in Lagos, Mrs. Olanike Ogundare was abducted from her home in the Oke Agaye area of Ijedodo by armed kidnappers around 1:00 a.m. The assailants overpowered the security gua...

item.title: Methods for Measuring Spacecraft Velocity in Deep Space
item.description: Determining speed in space is far more complex than on Earth. Unlike on Earth, where visual references like trees, cows, or GPS data help gauge velocity, spacecraft lack direct speed measurements. Spe...

item.title: Malaria in Africa: progress, vaccines, and ongoing challenges
item.description: Malaria continues to be one of the most serious public health problems in Africa, even though it is preventable and treatable. According to the World Malaria Report 2025, there were about 282 million ...

item.title: Darrell Sheets, ‘Storage Wars’ personality, dies at 67 in Arizona
item.description: Darrell Sheets, best known for his role on the popular reality television series Storage Wars, has died at the age of 67. According to the Lake Havasu City Police Department in Arizona, Sheets was fou...

item.title: Amogelang Maepa explores desire and memory in clay at Berman Contemporary
item.description: Amogelang Maepa’s latest exhibition, shown at Berman Contemporary in Cape Town’s V&A Waterfront Silo District, offers a deeply sensory and conceptual exploration of fleeting desire, emotional residue,...

item.title: Fuel Price Differences in East Africa Mainly Driven by Taxes and Government Policies
item.description: Recently, there is much discussion across East Africa about why fuel prices are so different from one country to another. Kenyan President William Ruto said the high fuel prices in Kenya are due to th...

item.title: British Indian voters in Harrow express growing support for Reform UK
item.description: Savitha Prakash, a first-generation immigrant from India and NHS doctor in Harrow, has become a prominent supporter of Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage. She draws parallels between Farage and Indian Pri...

item.title: Vigilante mob kills NYSC member in Benue after mistaken identity
item.description: Serious anger don spread for Benue State after members of a local vigilante group allegedly beat one serving NYSC corps member, Ben Agir, to death for Buruku Local Government Area. The young man, wey ...

item.title: Home Affairs Confirms Lesotho Citizens Still Need Passports to Enter South Africa
item.description: The Department of Home Affairs in South Africa has officially denied recent reports and social media posts claiming that Lesotho nationals can enter South Africa without valid passports. The departmen...
